performance.title: Performance Mode
performance.subtitle: Choose how the laptop balances power and performance

descriptions.silence: ~65 W burst (~34 s) / ~35 W sustained. Longest PL2 window of all modes. Fan at max during burst.
descriptions.balance: ~60 W burst (~11 s) / ~35 W sustained. Adaptive fan. Recommended for most workloads.
descriptions.turbo: ~62 W burst (~5 s) / ~15 W sustained. Short PL2 spike, then conservative PL1.
descriptions.smart: AI-adaptive. ~62 W burst (~5 s) / ~15 W base. Learns workload patterns over time.
descriptions.longBattery: ~60 W peak burst / ~42 W sustained. Moderate long-session power. Fan stays quiet.
descriptions.decepticon: ~35-40 W flat (no burst phase, stable 88°C). Requires firmware unlock.
descriptions.smartAcceleration: AI + burst detection. ~65 W burst (~12 s) / ~38 W base. Reacts to sudden load spikes.
descriptions.overdrive: Hidden firmware mode (ODV1=4). Measured: ~65-83 W burst (~17 s) / ~50 W sustained. Experimental.
descriptions.overdriveHigh: Hidden firmware mode (ODV1=5). Measured: ~62-67 W sustained (uncapped PL1). Experimental.
descriptions.overdriveMax: Hidden firmware mode (ODV1=6). Measured: ~60-77 W sustained (uncapped PL1). Experimental.
descriptions.smartAdaptive: EC-driven auto-scaling. ~65-73 W burst (~13 s) / ~35 W base. The embedded controller adjusts TDP dynamically.

techDetails.title: Technical Details
techDetails.overlayNote: Windows power mode changes automatically — this is expected behavior.
techDetails.aiLockedMsg: Configure an AI API key in Settings to unlock this mode.
techDetails.smartDiff: Both use AI to adapt TDP, but differ in reactivity. Smart adjusts gradually to sustained load (learns patterns over minutes); Smart Acceleration adds transient spike detection — reacts within seconds to sudden demands with a power burst before returning to the adaptive baseline. Use Smart for stable workloads; Smart Acceleration for mixed workflows with occasional intense loads.

modes.silence: Longest PL2 burst window of all modes (~34 s at ~65 W), then drops sharply to ~35 W PL1 (same sustained budget as Balance). Peak 99°C during burst, drops to ~80-83°C in PL1 phase. Despite the name, the fan runs at full speed during the burst. The extra-long PL2 Tau avoids burst-throttle oscillations — good for workloads that need a sustained spike longer than Balance (~11 s) but do not require permanent high TDP. WMI command: 0.
modes.balance: Default mode. CPU bursts to ~60 W for about 11 seconds (PL2), then settles to ~35 W sustained (PL1). Fan responds adaptively to load. Covers most workloads without excessive heat or noise. WMI command: 1.
modes.turbo: Short PL2 window (~5-6 s) at ~62 W, then drops sharply to ~15 W PL1. Peak CPU temp: 95°C. Despite the name, Turbo does NOT provide the highest sustained performance — Overdrive modes exceed it significantly. WMI command: 2.
modes.smart: Xiaomi AI engine analyzes CPU/GPU history and continuously adjusts between Silence and Turbo levels. Measured power profile matches Turbo: ~62 W burst (~5 s) / ~15 W sustained. Transitions are gradual — learns patterns before increasing power. WMI command: 10. Requires AI API key.
modes.longBattery: Unusual power profile: brief ~60 W burst (~2 s) then sustains ~42 W throughout. Peak temp 91°C. Does NOT cap to ultra-low power — sustained draw is moderate. WMI command: 11.
modes.decepticon: Flat sustained power with NO burst phase: ~35-40 W constant, peaking at only 88°C. Most thermally stable profile. Despite the dramatic name, it runs COOLER and more consistently than Balance or Turbo. Requires ACPI firmware unlock. WMI command: 3.
modes.smartAcceleration: Smart + transient spike detection. Burst profile: ~65 W for ~12 s, then sustains ~38 W. Unlike Smart (which only adapts to sustained load), detects sudden spikes and temporarily unlocks TDP near peak before returning to the adaptive baseline. WMI command: 14. Requires AI API key.
modes.overdrive: Hidden firmware profile ODV1=4. Measured: ~65-83 W burst (~17 s) / ~50 W sustained (100°C). Significantly higher sustained TDP than Turbo. Long PL2 window before settling to ~50 W PL1. WMI command: 4.
modes.overdriveHigh: Hidden firmware profile ODV1=5. Measured: ~62-67 W SUSTAINED throughout (no PL1 throttle detected in 25 s window). PL1 ≈ PL2 ≈ 62 W — effectively uncapped burst. Peak 99°C. The most consistent high-performance profile. WMI command: 5.
modes.overdriveMax: Hidden firmware profile ODV1=6 — highest DPTF index. Measured: ~60-77 W sustained throughout (uncapped PL1, 100°C). Nearly identical to Overdrive+ with slightly higher peak variance. WMI command: 6.
modes.smartAdaptive: EC-driven adaptive mode. QFAN=9 instructs the embedded controller to read the SMMT sub-register and auto-scale across the full ODV1 range. Measured: ~65-73 W burst (~13 s) / ~35 W sustained. Unlike Smart (fixed at ODV1=2), this auto-selects the power table. WMI command: 9.

infoModal.title: Battery Information
infoModal.levelDesc: Percentage of energy remaining relative to the current full-charge capacity.
infoModal.healthDesc: Ratio of the current full-charge capacity to the original designed capacity. Values ≥80% are normal for daily use.
infoModal.capacityDesc: Capacity is reported by ACPI in mWh (milliwatt-hours). Wh = mWh ÷ 1,000. mAh is estimated from the current voltage and may vary slightly with temperature.
infoModal.cyclesDesc: One full discharge (0→100%) counts as one cycle. Partial charges accumulate proportionally. Most lithium cells last 500–1,000 cycles before significant capacity loss.
infoModal.chargeRateDesc: Power flowing to or from the battery, measured by the battery driver in mW. This is the battery’s own charge or discharge rate — not the total AC adapter output. Adapter wattage = battery charge rate + system power draw, but system consumption is not available through standard Windows APIs.
infoModal.timeDesc: Instantaneous estimate based on current energy and power rate. When discharging on battery: remaining energy ÷ discharge rate. When charging: energy needed to fill ÷ charge rate. Fluctuates with varying workload.

charging.title: Charging Control
charging.subtitle: Limit maximum charge to extend battery lifespan
charging.protectModeDesc: Limits charging to 80% to extend battery lifespan

infoModal.title: How Charging Control Works
infoModal.functionDesc: Sends a threshold command via named pipe to the Xiaomi IoTService, which forwards it to the IoTDriver.sys kernel driver. The driver instructs the embedded controller (EC) to stop charging once the battery reaches the configured percentage.
infoModal.requiresDesc: Requires IoTDriver.sys (Xiaomi) to be installed and IoTService to be running. If the pipe connection fails, the value is stored in the registry and only takes effect when the service restarts.
infoModal.behaviorDesc: This is a charge ceiling — it does not discharge the battery. If you are already above the configured limit, the limit applies on the next charge cycle (discharge below the limit, then plug in).
infoModal.warningDesc: This feature was reverse-engineered from the Xiaomi IoTService binary (message type 0x1003). Values 40–80 are interpreted as charge limit percentages, but on some firmware variants the same command may control the charging current rate instead. If you observe the battery consistently charging past your configured limit, the IoTDriver may not be active on your device.

display.title: Display
display.igclNotAvailable: Requires Intel IGCL — not found on this device
display.sensitivityHelpMin: Min/Max — limits the brightness range regardless of light level.
display.sensitivityHelpLevel: Sensitivity — max brightness reached at ~{lux} lux. Lower = outdoors (bright), higher = indoors (dim).
display.sensitivityHelpSmoothing: Smoothing — transition speed: 0 = instant, 90 = very gradual (~6 s per step).

dialog.title: Telemetry & AI Features Consent
dialog.intro: MiControl can send hardware telemetry (device state, sensor data) to OpenAI to power AI-assisted features. This data helps provide intelligent hardware optimisation recommendations.
dialog.what: Hardware telemetry: battery level, CPU/GPU temperature, performance mode, touchpad state, display settings, and system information
dialog.where: OpenAI API — processed on their servers in accordance with their privacy policy
dialog.why: To provide AI-powered insights and hardware optimisation recommendations
dialog.control: You can revoke this consent at any time in Settings → Privacy. AI features will stop immediately.
dialog.sentryDisclosure: Crash reports will be sent to Sentry to help improve the app. No personal data is included.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.section1Body:
When you enable AI-powered features, MiControl collects hardware telemetry data from your device. This includes:

• Battery information: charge level, health percentage, cycle count, temperature, charging status, capacity
• CPU / GPU data: usage percentage, temperature, package power draw (TDP)
• Performance mode: current power profile and switching events
• Display information: brightness level, refresh rate, HDR status
• Fan state: speed (RPM), temperature readings
• Touchpad state: sensitivity, haptics configuration
• System information: RAM usage, OS version, device model

No personal identifying information (name, email, location, files) is collected unless explicitly provided by you.
privacy.section2Body: Hardware telemetry data is sent to OpenAI via their API (api.openai.com). If you have configured a custom endpoint (such as Ollama, Azure OpenAI, or LM Studio), data is sent to that endpoint instead. Data is transmitted over HTTPS and is not stored by MiControl on any external server.
privacy.section3Body:
The collected hardware data is used exclusively to:

• Analyse your system’s performance and battery health
• Provide personalised hardware optimisation recommendations
• Detect potential hardware issues or thermal anomalies
• Suggest optimal performance modes for your current workload

Data is not used for advertising, profiling, or sold to third parties.
privacy.section4Body: Telemetry data is sent to the AI provider in real time during an analysis request. MiControl does not persistently store the data you send. Performance logs written to disk (if AI Analysis logging is enabled) are stored locally on your device and are never automatically uploaded. You can clear these logs at any time from the AI Analysis tab.
privacy.section5Body:
You have full control over your data:

• Grant or deny consent at any time via Settings → Privacy
• Revoke previously granted consent — this immediately stops all telemetry transmission
• Delete locally stored performance logs from the AI Analysis tab
• Uninstall MiControl to remove all locally stored configuration and logs

If you have separately provided personal data to OpenAI, you must contact them directly to exercise your data rights under applicable law.
privacy.section6Body: For privacy-related inquiries about MiControl, please open an issue on the GitHub repository: github.com/Freitas-MA/miPC
